failure_surface: Agent implemented formulas but with incorrect source row indexing (picking header row instead of data), incomplete column coverage verification (missing last column R), and inconsistent alignment application (some columns showed 'center' instead of expected 'left' for Product names)
root_cause: Row boundary determination error and column range iteration mismatch: Agent started scanning for valid rows at index 4 which contained header content rather than data, resulting in incorrect formula references. Additionally, the verification loop used range(12, 18) which iterates 6 times (cols 12-17 = L-Q) instead of covering all 7 target columns (L-R = 12-18).
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before generating formulas, explicitly verify where data truly begins versus where headers end by inspecting row contents programmatically. Use inclusive range boundaries that match the task's answer_position dimensions exactly.
evidence: Verification output showed Row 4 was included as first product with quantity units, but spreadsheet content shows Row 4 contains 'CODE PRODUCT' header text, not actual product data
Verification loop range(12, 18) produces columns L-M-N-O-P-Q (6 columns) but answer_position L5:R8 requires 7 columns (L through R)
Alignment output shows 'center' for columns L and N despite instructions requesting specific left/right alignment patterns
Formula references use static row numbers from detected list rather than dynamic filtering logic

memory_items.title: Validate Data Start Rows vs Header Rows
memory_items.lesson: When locating data rows programmatically, explicitly check whether scanned rows contain header content or actual data before using their indices in formulas
memory_items.generalization: Always verify row content type before including in data processing loops - look for numeric values, known patterns, or skip rows that contain column labels

memory_items.title: Match Column Range Boundaries to Target Specification
memory_items.lesson: Python range() is exclusive at the end; ensure iteration ranges cover all required columns by adding 1 to the maximum column index when converting from A1 notation to column numbers
memory_items.generalization: When working with A1 notation ranges, calculate exact column count and ensure verification/writing loops iterate over the complete target range inclusively

memory_items.title: Verify Alignment Application Completeness
memory_items.lesson: After applying style changes, validate that alignment properties are set on every intended cell, not just selectively
memory_items.generalization: Iterate through all target cells when applying formatting rules to prevent partial styling that leaves some cells with default or unexpected alignment values

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons

### Row Boundary Validation
- Before using row indices in formulas, programmatically verify that rows contain actual data (numbers, product codes) rather than header labels. Headers typically appear in fixed positions and should be skipped.
- When data starts after headers, add appropriate offset to row counting logic.

### Column Range Iteration Precision
- Python range() excludes its stop value; if targeting columns L-R (7 columns), use range(start_col, end_col + 1) where end_col corresponds to R.
- Always cross-check that loop iterations match the A1 notation dimensions specified in task requirements.

### Complete Formatting Coverage
- Apply alignment, width, and other styles to all cells within the target range during formula writing, not just select cells.
- Validate formatting post-application to catch any cells left with default values.
